title: The AI layoff wave is becoming a powder keg
slides: Why are layoffs suddenly accelerating in AI firms?
Investors demand profits over headcount growth this quarter.
Yet those same cuts are killing internal innovation pipelines.
Your team's best projects now depend on surviving colleagues.
Protect your domain knowledge before the next round hits.
thread_text:
1. The innovation trap

AI firms slashed R&D teams by 20% this quarter to please shareholders.

2. Profits first, ideas last

Investor pressure is forcing CEOs to choose between next quarter's earnings and next year's breakthrough.

3. The brain drain effect

Every layoff removes not just people but the informal knowledge that powered your best projects.

4. Survivors carry the load

Your AI model's success now depends on overworked colleagues who learned from those let go.

5. Map your expertise today

List the undocumented workflows only you know. If you leave, your team loses them.
video_script: Protect your knowledge before next round.

thread:

2. Profits first, ideas last

Investor pressure is forcing CEOs to choose between next quarter's earnings and next year's breakthrough.

3. The brain drain effect

4. Survivors carry the load

Your AI model's success now depends on overworked colleagues who learned from those let go.

5. Map your expertise today

script: Protect your knowledge before next round.

instagram.caption:
Your AI team just got cut by 20% this quarter—and the same executives demanding those cuts also expect you to ship your next breakthrough.

Profit pressure from investors is forcing CEOs to pick between this quarter's earnings and next year's innovation. Every layoff removes the undocumented shortcuts, failed experiments, and domain knowledge your best projects were built on.

If you're still on payroll, your success now relies on overworked colleagues forced to carry what the let-go team knew.

What undocumented workflow or insight exists only in your head right now—and what happens when you're the one let go next?
